does: This paper introduces DLPO, a reinforcement learning approach specifically designed to fine-tune diffusion-based text-to-speech models using predicted mean opinion scores (MOS) as a reward signal. The method adapts RLHF techniques from image synthesis to the speech domain, leveraging the UTokyo-SaruLab MOS prediction system as a proxy loss function to guide policy optimization. The authors evaluate DLPO against alternative RLHF approaches using both automated metrics from the NISQA speech quality assessment model and human preference evaluations, demonstrating whether RL-based optimization can improve the quality and naturalness of synthesized speech compared to baseline diffusion models.
connects: This work builds on the emerging research applying RLHF to diffusion models, following successful applications in image synthesis demonstrated by Lee et al., Black et al., Wang et al., and Fan et al. It addresses a significant gap by investigating whether these RLHF techniques transfer to speech synthesis, where architectural differences between image and audio diffusion models create uncertainty about effectiveness. The paper connects to the broader literature on using human feedback to steer generative models and leverages existing quality prediction systems like NISQA and MOS estimation to provide reward signals without requiring extensive human annotation, bridging diffusion model optimization with reinforcement learning methodology.
enables: This work opens directions for improving speech synthesis quality through learned reward optimization, potentially extending RLHF strategies to other speech-related tasks beyond text-to-speech such as voice conversion or speech enhancement. It establishes a practical framework for using automated quality metrics as proxy rewards in RL fine-tuning, which could reduce human annotation costs while maintaining quality improvements. The findings may inform how architectural differences between modalities affect the transferability of RLHF techniques, guiding future applications of reinforcement learning to diffusion models in other domains. Results could also motivate development of better MOS prediction systems tailored specifically for RL-based optimization loops.
